definition: The governing coalition's most federally dependent electoral base ($1.30-$1.80 received per tax dollar) creates a political economy paradox for DOGE: actual spending cuts would disproportionately harm the political support base, with a 12-18 month feedback cycle from cuts to electoral consequences.
evidence: Most federally dependent states receive $1.30-$1.80 per tax dollar paid
These states constitute the governing coalition's electoral base
12-18 month political feedback cycle from federal dependency to electoral consequences
Historical realization rate of announced cuts: 0.3-0.6x
open_questions: How does the 12-18 month feedback cycle manifest in midterm election dynamics?
Can DOGE target spending cuts to avoid politically sensitive constituencies?
